pages.raw_text:
Officers were elected last night at
the meeting Of the Falls City Trades
and Labor Assembly to serve for the
ensuing six months. Before the cleo
ton began President Joyce said:


1--WE congratulate ourselves on the
growth of the Falls City Trades and
Labor assembly. We started our or
ganization with only 10 unions. Now
we have II unions that are affiliated
with us, an dali Of them are in good
standing. 1t can be said that our or
ganization is composed of the most
conservative union men in the city.
On our withdrawal from the Spokane
trades council we eliminated all dis
cussion Of politics from our assembly,
and that has been the great secret
of our growth.


I'VE have accomplished great
deal during the last six months Of
our existence. We have settled sew
eral labor disputes and hope to settle
many more. We have an average
attendance Of from 2S to 80 delegates
Of the unions. but should have 80 or
60 delegates"


The meeting was harmonious ex-
cept when President Joyce roasted
the Spokane Record because of recent
untrue statements. The paper was
repudiated as an official organ.


These officers were elected: Presi-
dent, Thomas Joyce Of the bartenders'
union; vice president, William Quinn
of the machinists' union; correspond
ng and recording secretary, George
Cummings Of the walters union;
financial secretary, H. s. Wilkinson of
the cigarmakers union; treasurer,
John Hunt Of the stationary engi-
neers.
